text: You have three hours (plus any pre-agreed individual adjustment) to complete the paper.
Answer four essay questions, TWO from Section A and TWO from Section B.
Each essay question carries the same number of marks.
Candidates are advised to devote equal time to each question.

This is a closed book exam.
Candidates should not copy and paste images from any other documents.
Where appropriate (e.g. for formulae, equations, or diagrams) you may hand write or draw on the Inspera paper provided.

sub-instructions: ⓘ Answer TWO questions from SECTION A and TWO questions from SECTION B.
content: Explain how transplantation experiments helped unravel the mechanisms of immunological tolerance.
Describe how antigens are presented on class I and class II major histocompatibility complexes (MHC) and discuss consequences of MHC polymorphism.
Using examples, describe how viruses evade host immune responses.
Describe the replication cycle of influenza virus. How do avian influenza viruses adapt to spread in human populations?
Using examples, describe how bacterial virulence effector protein injection systems contribute to pathogenesis.
With reference to parasite biology and host responses, discuss the challenges in developing vaccines to prevent malaria (Plasmodium falciparum) and river blindness (Onchocerca volvulus).
“Atherogenesis is a chronic inflammatory process”. Discuss.
Discuss how environmental mutagens lead to cancer development. How is this process influenced by other contributory factors, including inflammation?
